question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks essentially no didactic questions. It presents a complete solution upfront and only responds to questions the student raises. There are no teachable-moment prompts back to the user.

question_quality.rationale: No substantive choices are framed for the user. The Pair makes all design decisions itself (regex pattern, flush approach, character class) without inviting the student to reason through any of them.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no question sequence to scaffold. The Pair does respond well to the student's questions with a nice trace and ambiguity discussion, but this is reactive explanation, not pedagogical scaffolding.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and explanations are clear and actionable. The flush trace and digit-ambiguity table are particularly well-targeted to the student's questions, producing smooth back-and-forth, even if not Socratic.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code works correctly on all round-trip tests, comparable to baseline. Uses manual loop instead of baseline's groupby, but functionally equivalent.

friction.rationale: Two turns, ~56s — essentially identical to baseline's 2 turns/62s. Slight extra pedagogical payoff from the digit-ambiguity discussion balances the more verbose initial dump.
